entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 24 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-24 07:30AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Blues on the hunt for top-four spot after downing Giants
  • Lollipop lady hangs up her whistle after 31 years of service
  • ‘Radically anti-union’: Qld Labor accused of trying to freeze out new worker groups
  • Unis face new cheating threat as regulator prepares crackdown
  • The slump in metal prices offers a golden opportunity for savvy investors
  • Bitcoin buyers outrank sellers despite brutal market crash
  • Use of epidural kits limited as global shortage hits Australian hospitals
  • Push to transform the ‘desolate’ spaces beneath Melbourne’s roads and rail
  • Simmons faces a ‘challenging’ NBA return, says teammate Curry
  • Australia joins outrage over ‘barbaric’ strike on food deal in Odesa
  • Tino complains about ‘eye scrape’, Addo-Carr targeted with bottle in Bulldogs’ win
  • Raising Warragamba dam needs federal money, says Perrottet
  • Best of cartoons, July 24, 2022
  • Albanese backs ‘strongest ever’ foot and mouth measures, warns against border closure
  • Labor winding back powers of building watchdog to ‘bare minimum’
  • Why we can’t seem to quit the Austen-Brontes
  • Crossbench crucial in 47th parliament – but first, pomp and ceremony
  • Greens’ Bandt demands block on funding for coal and gas, flags budget fight
  • Medicare abortion rebates needed to end the ‘postcode lottery’: MSI
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • Best of cartoons, July 23, 2022
  • The Splendour of gumboots in a world gone to mud
  • Man charged with murder after missing woman’s body found dumped at Dural
  • Boy, 10, perishes in third death from ferocious Sydney house fire
  • Disaster capitalism: Business has found an upside to fires and floods
  • Traffic chaos and building plans send locals to war with private school
  • Averse to poetry? It’s no surprise
  • Genetic banks and cleaning mats: Preparing for the arrival of foot and mouth
  • Emergency declared as wildfire erupts near Yosemite
  • Two years after drive began, China reveals Xi Jinping received local COVID vaccine
  • ‘Detecting the most interesting thing’: NASA looks to use satellites to search for aliens
  • Joe Biden has sore throat and body aches, but ‘COVID symptoms improving’
  • ‘In the spotlight’: Climate boss warns Australia needs to lift its climate ambitions
  • WHO declares monkeypox a health emergency as infections soar
  • Ukraine says Russian missiles hit Odesa port, putting grain deal at risk
  • Into thin air: Why is Australia facing a gas crisis?
  • Robin Dalton: spy, socialite, film producer and author
  • Qld health authorities say ‘no need to be alarmed’ about monkeypox
  • From the Archives, 1992: The State Library sells off its treasures
  • Villa force late draw against Man United
  • ‘Incredibly thankful’: Cameron Smith awarded Brisbane’s highest honour
  • As Omicron strains spread, is this the forever plague?
  • Australia’s first crypto exchange is not a typical success story
  • Shoppers warned to ‘think twice’ about online retailer under investigation
  • ‘Underpaid and under-educated’: Call to boost qualifications and pay of early educators
  • Exit strategy: Meet the grape growers still battling China’s wine tariffs
  • Crowdfunding reprieve for Earlwood pensioners facing strata bankruptcy
  • Festival attendees forced to wait overnight for buses in crowded, muddy lines
  • ‘Massive pressure’: fantasy franchises go to war at Comic-Con
  • Queenie van de Zandt in Blue: The Songs Of Joni Mitchell
  • Ramsay St to the world: The Neighbours who owe their careers to a TV soap
  • Liam Gallagher review: An oasis of pleasure in expat city
  • ‘She became a mother figure to me’: Matty Mills on working with Catherine Martin
  • Three decades after Mabo, his daughter immortalises the maps that changed history
  • Bangarra’s powerful story of loss, and the black divas who changed the world
  • ‘Keep marrying until you find love’: the celebrities who keep walking down the aisle
  • Estranged Aitkens play nice for Amalfi Coast family reunion
  • Why your friends are disappearing from your Instagram feed
  • ‘Worse than tobacco’: Climate activists push for ban on fossil fuel ads
  • Crackdown on environmental crimes as prosecutions up threefold in five years
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 24 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-24 07:08AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Khmer Rouge survivor in the US opens up on escaping genocide in father-daughter podcast

  • Joe Biden has sore throat and body aches, but Covid-19 symptoms continue to improve, says doctor
  • WHO declares monkeypox a global emergency, sounding the health agency’s highest alarm
  • Taps running dry in Mexico’s wealthy city of Monterrey; ‘like we’ve gone back in time’
  • Greece tourist resort evacuated as wildfire destroys Lesbos island homes
  • Russian missiles hit Ukraine’s Odesa, ‘Putin’s spit in the face’ for grain deal
  • US reports first monkeypox cases in children
  • Heed the warnings of a global economic crisis from those in the know – and ignore those saying everything’s fine
  • Life coach reveals how a near-death experience shipwrecked at sea gave her the courage to change course
  • For the love of the game: the Hong Kong athletes who risked career, family and Covid-19 to achieve their sporting dreams
  • Who is Tia Ray? Meet the Chinese singer with one of the bestselling singles in the world
  • Brigitte Lin’s first gender-neutral role, Sally Yeh’s butch Beijing Opera performer make Tsui Hark’s 1986 period comedy Peking Opera Blues highly enjoyable
  • What drew three women to bear Elon Musk children besides his wealth? Women’s reproductive priorities explained
  • Alchemy of Souls renewed for second season; Train to Busan, Hellbound director returns to Netflix – K-drama casting latest
  • From 30kg overweight and depressed to Ironman competitor – how a Singapore expat addicted to alcohol and junk food turned his life around
  • How to pack light and avoid lost or delayed baggage this summer – curate your wardrobe and fit everything in a carry-on bag
  • Serving Chinese food as an entrée to East Asian culture, cafe owner from Hong Kong hopes to get people of Birmingham, UK, talking
  • 6 British royals who survived attempted assassinations: Queen Elizabeth, Prince Charles and Queen Victoria have been fired at – while Princess Anne famously resisted her would-be kidnapper
  • Inside Pippa Middleton and James Matthews’ happy family: Kate Middleton’s sister and her billionaire husband just had their third baby … so what’s their married life like?
  • Inside Donald and Ivana Trump’s ‘starter’ mansion: they bought the 20,000 sq ft Connecticut home after getting married, then Ivana won it in their divorce settlement before selling it for millions
  • Meet Anna Weyant, the ‘millennial Botticelli’ making millions: the Canadian-born artist rose to fame on Instagram and was signed by Gagosian – but is she really dating a 77-year-old gallery founder?
  • Meet Frédéric Arnault, the Tag Heuer CEO vying to run LVMH: Bernard Arnault’s third son bagged Ryan Gosling as brand ambassador, plays tennis with Roger Federer and launched an NFT-friendly watch
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Unexploded wartime bomb defused; JR West resumes services
  • COVID-19 cases top 200,000 across Japan as BA.5 strain rages
  • High COVID-19 counts in Tokyo, Osaka, Okinawa and other areas
  • Todai to offer metaverse studies to teach engineering
  • Suspect in Abe slaying ordered to undergo psychiatric tests
  • Owner closes 115-year-old ‘soba’ shop because his kids are allergic
  • Kishida wanted rationale for holding state funeral for Abe
  • SKorea to lift ban on NKorea TV, newspapers despite tensions
  • U.N. health chief says monkeypox is now a global emergency
  • Ukraine and Russia seal grain deal as U.S. pledges more military aid
  • Team fuses AI, robotics to decide ideal cultivation step for iPS cells
  • Global warming study: 5-year droughts could become the norm
  • Comic C'moA digital manga site set to build market abroad
  • EDITORIAL: Diplomacy must be a vital key in strengthening Japan’s security
  • VOX POPULI: Living in a world where inflation will be with us for years to come
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
